ORDER DENYING DEFENDANTS' MOTION TO QUASH OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, FOR PROTECTIVE ORDER

DISCOVERY 302, DEFENDANTS MISSION NEIGHBORHOOD HEALTH, BRENDA STOREY, CHARLES MOSER AND FERNANDO-GOMEZ-BENITEZ'S MOTION TO QUASH, OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, MOTION FOR PROTETIVE ORDER OF PLAINTIFF'S SUBPOENA FOR THE DEPOSITIONS OF RAFAEL ILDEFONZO, R. CHRISTOPH SADOVAL, AND JOANNA DEVITO-LARSON, AND SUBPOENA FOR DOCUMENTS THEREON - THE MOTION TO QUASH AND/OR FOR PROTECTIVE ORDER IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF SHOWED THAT AT LEAST TWO OF THE NONPARTIES HAD NO OBJECTION TO THE SUBPOENA, AND THAT THE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENTS WOULD NOT BE VIOLATED BY COMPLYING. AND, AS PLAINTIFF POINTS OUT, THE NONPARTIES' CLAIMS WERE NOT LIMITED TO "WHISTLEBLOWER" CLAIMS, BUT ALSO INCLUDED MANY OF THE CLAIMS ASSERTED HERE. THEIR KNOWLEDGE IS THUS RELEVANT, NOTWITHSTANDING THE FACT THAT THEY MAY HAVE LEFT EMPLOYMENT BEFORE THE ALLEGED WRONGFUL TREATMENT OF PLAINTIFF. THE SUBPOENAS WERE THUS ISSUED IN GOOD FAITH. DEFENDANTS' MOTION FOR A PROTECTIVE ORDER IS DENIED AS WELL. THE MATTERS DESCRIBED AS CONFIDENTIAL UNDER THE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENTS ARE LIMITED TO THE AMOUNT, TERMS AND CONDITIONS, AND NEGOTIATIONS PERTAINING TO THE SETTLEMENT. PLAINTIFF MAY EXAMINE THE WITNESSES ON ANY OTHER TOPICS. FURTHERMORE, THE CONFIDENTIALITY PROVISIONS DO NOT APPLY TO TESTIMONY ADDUCED PURSUANT TO A SUBPOENA. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. PRO TEM JUDGE: JIM WEIXEL, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT, NOT REPORTED. =302/JPT

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF CINTYA GONZALEZ'S MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION OF ORDER GRANTING MOTION FOR SANCTIONS IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION DOES NOT OFFER ANY NEW FACTS, OR AT LEAST ONES THAT DO NOT CONTRADICT THE SWORN EVIDENCE ALREADY IN THE RECORD FROM PLAINTIFF, AND NO NEW CIRCUMSTANCES OR LAW. (CCP 1008(A).) ALTHOUGH PLAINTIFF IS CORRECT THAT THE NOTICE OF MOTION DID NOT SPECIFY THAT DEFENDANT SOUGHT SANCTIONS AGAINST PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL, IN ADDITION TO PLAINTIFF, DEFENDANT'S MOVING PAPERS DID. APPEARANCES - ATTY. GENER D. BENITEZ OF THE IBARRA LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. 398-5329 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF CINTYA GONZALEZ's Motion To Tax Costs is granted in part. The Court concludes that the motion is timely based on defendant's proof of service that indicated mail service. See CCP 12a. The Court strikes the $60 filing fee for the untimely motion to strike. The Court denies the motion as to the videotaping costs. Such costs are recoverable under CCP 1033.5(a)(3) and Plaintiff fails to show that the costs were not reasonably necessary to the litigation. Notice Of Motion And Motion For Reconsideration Of Order Granting Motion For Sanctions
Off Calendar Jul-19-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: crisostomo ibarra, esq., 415.398.5329, for plaintiff cintya gonzalez.
natalja fulton, esq., 415.262.8589, for defendant mission neighborhood health center.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: PLAINTIFF CINTYA GONZALEZ's Motion For Reconsideration Of Order Granting Motion For Sanctions is Denied. Plaintiff's motion does not offer any new facts, or at least ones that do not contradict the sworn evidence already in the record from plaintiff, and no new circumstances or law. (CCP 1008(a).) Although Plaintiff is correct that the notice of motion did not specify that Defendant sought sanctions against plaintiff's counsel, in addition to plaintiff, defendant's moving papers did. Further, Defendant expressly sought the lesser alternative sanction of an adverse inference. The monetary sanctions of $ 1,500 were issued within the discretion of the Court. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: joan martin, csr #6036, 415.421.3021.

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT MODIFIED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND RULED AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANTS MISSION NEIGHBORHOOD HEALTH, BRENDA STOREY, CHARLES MOSER, FERNANDO-GOMEZ-BENITEZ'S Motion To Quash, Or In The Alternative, Motion For Protetive Order Of Plaintiff's Subpoena For The Depositions Of Rafael Ildefonzo, R. Christoph Sadoval, And Joanna Devito-Larson, And Subpoena For Documents Thereon - The motion to quash and/or for protective order is denied. Plaintiff showed that at least two of the nonparties had no objection to the subpoena, and that the settlement agreements would not be violated by complying. And, as Plaintiff points out, the nonparties' claims were not limited to "whistleblower" claims, but also included many of the claims asserted here. Their knowledge is thus relevant, notwithstanding the fact that they may have left employment before the alleged wrongful treatment of Plaintiff. The subpoenas were thus issued in good faith.
Defendants' motion for a protective order is denied as well. The matters described as confidential under the settlement agreements are limited to the amount, terms and conditions, and negotiations pertaining to the settlement. Plaintiff may examine the witnesses on any other topics. Furthermore, the confidentiality provisions do not apply to testimony adduced pursuant to a subpoena. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT.
